title: Unconventional Magnetism: Symmetry Classification, Hybrid-parity and Unconstrained-parity Classes
authors: Xun-Jiang Luo, Dan Li, Rui-Chun Xiao, Ding-Fu Shao, Lei Li, Mingliang Tian, Yugui Yao
abstract: Unconventional magnetism has emerged as a transformative frontier in condensed matter physics. Such phases are characterized by substantial non-relativistic spin splitting (NSS) in symmetry-compensated magnets. They have been classified by the parity of their spin textures under momentum inversion, leading to the paradigms of altermagnets (even-parity) and odd-parity magnets. However, the full symmetry landscape remains largely unexplored. In this Letter, we present a systematic classification framework for unconventional magnetism based on the representation theory of the spin textures and the associated parity properties. Within this framework, we predict two previously unidentified classes beyond the established pure-parity categories: hybrid-parity magnets (HPMs) and unconstrained-parity magnets (UPMs), where the spin textures exhibit contrasting parities among their Cartesian components and the parity of the spin textures is ill-defined, respectively. We derive universal symmetry criteria that categorize HPMs into three distinct types. Importantly, by combining the spin splitting characteristics of altermagnets and odd-parity magnets, HPMs can enable the coexistence of the spin current and Edelstein effects. Taking FePO4 as an example, we perform first-principles calculations to demonstrate this coexistence. Finally, we discuss the potential applications of HPMs in spintronic devices. Our work provides a comprehensive symmetry classification of unconventional magnetism and establishes HPMs as a promising platform for multi-functional spintronics.
summary: This paper establishes a new symmetry-based classification for unconventional magnetism, predicting the existence of hybrid-parity and unconstrained-parity magnets. It demonstrates that hybrid-parity magnets allow for the simultaneous occurrence of spin current and Edelstein effects, offering a new platform for multifunctional spintronic devices.
main_problem: The paper seeks to complete the symmetry taxonomy of unconventional magnetism beyond the established even-parity (altermagnets) and odd-parity paradigms.
main_result: The authors identify two new magnetic classes, hybrid-parity magnets (HPMs) and unconstrained-parity magnets (UPMs), and demonstrate that HPMs enable the coexistence of spin current and Edelstein effects.
method: The study uses Spin Space Group (SSG) theory for symmetry classification, combined with first-principles DFT calculations and tight-binding modeling to validate transport properties.
model_or_system: The study focuses on symmetry-compensated magnets, using FePO4 as a prototypical example of a Type-I hybrid-parity magnet, and explores candidate materials identified via the MAGNDATA database.
key_observables: Non-relativistic spin splitting (NSS), spin current conductivity, Edelstein susceptibility, and spin texture parity.
important_parameters: Spin texture dimensionality (collinear, coplanar, noncoplanar), spin-splitting group (SSG) symmetries, and effective U parameter for Fe-3d orbitals.
main_assumptions: The classification assumes the validity of the Spin Space Group framework and uses the constant relaxation-time approximation for transport calculations.
figures_summary: Figure 1 illustrates the four classes of unconventional magnetism and the decomposition of spin textures; Figure 2 provides a symmetry classification flowchart; Figure 3 shows the crystal structure and Fermi surface of FePO4; Figure 4 depicts proposed spintronic device architectures.
paper_structure: The paper begins with a symmetry-based classification of unconventional magnetism, introduces the new HPM and UPM classes, derives symmetry criteria for HPM types, validates these findings with DFT calculations on FePO4, and concludes with proposed spintronic applications.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. (a) Schematic illustration of the even-parity, odd-parity, hybrid-parity, and unconstrained-parity classes of UM. ˜P denotes the SSG symmetry that flips momentum. S(k) = (sx(k), sy(k), sz(k)) is the spin expectation value. E(k) is the energy at momentum k. (b) Illustration of a hybrid-parity spin texture and its decomposition into d-wave and p-wave channels along different spin directions, thereby leading to the spin current (js) and Edelstein effect under an applied electric field E.

FIG. 2. Flowchart illustrating the symmetry classification of UM. Starting from the SSG G, the derived point group Ps and G1 dictate the conditions for SCM and NSS, respectively. The spin textures form a representation of the point group ˜G. If ˜G is a Laue group, the spin texture acquires a definite parity, leading to even-, odd-, or hybrid-parity classes; if not, the unconstrained-parity class arises. Here λi = det(XiUi).

FIG. 3. (a) Crystal structure and magnetic order of bulk FePO4. (b) Fermi surface of FePO4 at EF = −35 meV. The spin textures on the kx = ±0.5 Å−1 planes are plotted; arrows indicate the orientation of vector S(k), and the color encodes the out-of-plane spin component sx(k). (c) Calculated T-odd spin Hall conductivity σz(o) xy and σz(o) yx as a function of the Fermi energy EF . (d) T-even Edelstein susceptibility χ(e) xx as a function of EF .

FIG. 4. Proposed spintronic device architectures enabled by HPMs. (a) Hybrid spin-splitter torque: a charge current in- jected into an HPM/FM bilayer generates spin currents with multi spin polarizations components. (b) Magnetic tunnel junction: the top layer utilizes the Edelstein effect for electri- cal write operations, while the bottom HPM layer provides a spin-polarized readout current. (c) Spin transistor: an HPM channel sandwiched between ferromagnetic (FM) source (S) and drain (D) contacts; gate voltage Vtg modulates spin pre- cession in the HPM, enabling non-volatile logic functionality.
